message:
fix: Handle *PyObject in len_builtin for runtime.eval results

The len_builtin function didn't handle *PyObject types returned by
runtime.eval(). When eval() returns a list, len() would incorrectly
return 0 because *PyObject fell through to the fallback case.

Added check for ptr_info.child == cpython.PyObject to dispatch to
pyobject_utils.pyLen() which properly extracts length from PyList,
PyDict, PyTuple, and PyString objects.

This fixes test_longexp which creates a large list via eval() and
checks its length.
